# Solution

## (a) Implementation of Canonicalize

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, max(x.width, PAD))
}
```

where `max(a, b) = if a >= b then a else b`.

Alternatively, without a helper:

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}
```

## (b) Ensures clauses for Spec

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          y.kind == x.kind
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          y.value == x.value
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          y.width >= PAD
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          Wellformed(y)
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          (x.width >= PAD ==> y.width == x.width)
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          (x.width < PAD ==> y.width == PAD)
{ }
```

Or equivalently, written more concisely:

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == x.width
  ensures x.width < PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == PAD
{ }
```

**Key properties captured:**
1. Kind and value are preserved (the entity never changes)
2. Width becomes at least `PAD` (4 digits minimum)
3. The result is wellformed
4. Ids already at or above 4 digits are unchanged
5. Ids below 4 digits are padded exactly to 4
